top_signals.title: UK financial regulators to begin overseeing Critical Third Parties announced by HM Treasury
top_signals.summary: The Bank of England, the Prudential Regulation Authority and the Financial Conduct Authority will start overseeing the first Critical Third Parties on Monday 13 July 2026, following designation by HM Treasury.
top_signals.why_it_matters: Central-bank repricing affecting yields, FX, liquidity and equities.

top_signals.title: Policy paper: Update on the Digital Gilt Instrument (DIGIT) pilot issuance
top_signals.summary: The Chancellor announced that DIGIT will be issued by Q1 2027, and the government is taking steps to prepare for potential future issuances. HSBC and LSEG have signed an MoU to develop connectivity together to support investor access.
top_signals.why_it_matters: Central-bank repricing affecting yields, FX, liquidity and equities.

top_signals.title: Statutory guidance: Public Service Pensions and Judicial Offices Act: Amending Directions - July 2026
top_signals.summary: Amendments to Treasury Directions made under the Public Service Pensions and Judicial Offices Act 2022 in relation to the McCloud remedy.

top_signals.title: August 2019: Two Trucks and a Fresh Start. Today: Nearly 40 Trucks, 140 Trailers, and a 40-Year-Old Carrier.
top_signals.summary:
I have known Jerry Murphy for almost two years. We talk every couple of weeks. And I can tell you there is no version of him for the cameras. The man you hear on this episode is the same man I get on those calls. He did not have a driver’s license when he started […]

top_signals.title: Homebuyer affordability slipped for fifth straight month, real estate index shows
top_signals.summary: With both home prices and mortgage rates climbing since earlier this year, affording a home has become harder, according to a housing affordability index.

top_signals.title: Nasdaq Futures Tumble 1.9% as Chip Rout Deepens: Markets Wrap
top_signals.summary: Nasdaq 100 futures tumbled 1.9% as the global selloff in chipmakers gathered pace, with traders rushing to exit positions in stocks that had fueled this year’s rally but whose valuations grew increasingly rich.

top_signals.title: Baltic Dry Index falls to 2752 down 88 points
top_signals.summary: Today, Friday, July 17 2026, the Baltic Dry Index decreased by 88 points, reaching 2752 points. Baltic Dry Index is compiled by the London-based Baltic Exchange and covers prices for transported cargo such as coal, grain and iron ore. The index is based on a daily survey of agents all over the world. Baltic Dry ...
